content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be reconfigured to act as a 'curiosity accumulation scaffold' that resolves a longstanding flaw of intrinsic reward methods like ICM and RND: catastrophic forgetting of previously explored surprising states, which eliminates the cyclic re-discovery of trivial novelties that wastes agent exploration compute. This memory scaffold prioritizes storing out-of-distribution prediction errors (the core signal curiosity methods use) instead of general task experiences, turning sparse intrinsic reward signals into a cumulative, searchable knowledge base for targeted active exploration.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
